title: Traits I Value
author: Andrew Bosworth
paragraphs: I like people to think completely and exhaustively, to understand all their alternatives, all their assumptions, and all their limitations.
If someone else owns it, then it shouldn't occupy any of my standing mental space.

I value people who understand the cost of gathering information and the cost of the passage of time. We must work with great urgency to make progress, and often that means recognizing that the marginal value of more information is below the cost of delay.

Not only are we planning to succeed, but that the success we achieve will be one we value. This cannot exist without a long-term focus on consumers from whom all defensible value stems. This also requires an earnest ability to assess our own strengths and weaknesses.

I care about writing, in-meeting, and public communication abilities. Without these, nothing is scalable. If you have the capacity but aren't making the time to actually do the communicating, that isn't much better.
If someone is great but can't work with everyone else on the team, then they are of limited long-term use to the organization.
The people I work with need to have the confidence to show their work in an unfinished state and expose it to scrutiny, trusting in themselves and in the process that the results will be better.

I would trade almost every trait for every person who works for me to be self-aware about their faults, zealous about uncovering new faults, and rigorous about improving. This includes graciously accepting feedback when given, even if given imperfectly.
